headlines.title: Senate Republicans reject Iran war powers resolution after clashing with Trump at Capitol meeting
headlines.description: Republican senators switched their votes on an Iran war resolution late Wednesday, hours after a fractious meeting that included a shouting match with President Donald Trump over their opposition to the conflict

headlines.title: Michael Whatley’s Michigan roots largely disappear from his bio as he runs for Senate in North Carolina
headlines.description: Former RNC Chair Michael Whatley moved to North Carolina when he was in high school. He frequently says he is a “son” of the state and “grew up” there.

headlines.title: Civil rights, moon landings and war wins: What Americans see as the country’s greatest achievements
headlines.description: The NBC News poll asked American adults which historical event they thought was the country’s biggest accomplishment in its 250 years.

headlines.title: Senate Reverses Course on War Powers After Visit From Trump
headlines.description: In a major reversal, Senate Republicans refused to rein in President Donald Trump’s war powers, hours after he chastised members of his own party for going against him. Earlier in the day, the president refused to take part in a planned ceremony to sign a bill overwhelmingly passed by both parties aimed at making housing affordable, until, he says, the Senate passes the SAVE Act, which would require voters show proof of ID and citizenship documents to vote. NBC’s Kelly O’Donnell reports fo

headlines.title: Trump hints at possible military action beyond Iran and Venezuela
headlines.description: During his remarks at the National Mall to kick off the Great American State Fair, President Trump suggested the possibility of more military actions beyond those in Iran and the U.S. operation capturing Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro. Trump said, “And I guess we have other things in store, but we don’t want to get carried away.”

headlines.title: Chief of staff to former NYC mayor Eric Adams arrested by FBI in alleged bribery scheme
headlines.description: Frank Carone, former chief of staff to New York City Mayor Eric Adams, has been arrested by the FBI in connection with an alleged bribery-related scheme. According to the indictment, Carone and three others, including his brother, allegedly accepted more than $100,000 in bribes in exchange for steering a contract for an emergency shelter for migrants to a hotel in Queens. NBC New York's Jonathan Dienst reports.

headlines.title: Americans believe they aren’t being taught enough about democracy
headlines.description: For Thomas Jefferson, James Madison and other bold-face names struggling to bring about a new nation, an educated and informed citizenry was essential to the success of the American experiment.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Lawmakers expected to press billionaire Leon Black about Epstein ties
headlines.description: The phrase “Please call Leon Black” appears more than 300 times in the investigative files released by the Justice Department involving Epstein, a convicted sex offender.

headlines.title: Trump kicks off the Great American State Fair after musical acts pulled out
headlines.description: A number of musical acts backed out of the event on the National Mall last month, concerned about being part of something that appeared political.

headlines.title: Bipartisan housing affordability bill in limbo after Trump demands passage of SAVE Act
headlines.description: Hours before heading to Capitol Hill, Trump called the big, bipartisan housing bill Republicans hope to run on in the fall “of minor importance.” Then, he canceled his plan to sign it.
